$note: Captured snapshot of the COMPARED forecast/intelligence product for the azimuth benchmark (synthesis/benchmark.py). Quoted by product + capture date; NOT surfaced as an azimuth channel and NOT an L1 source. Refresh with pull_benchmark_foils.py.

foil.title: Oil price impact from Strait of Hormuz disruption
foil.scenario_short: Strait of Hormuz risk: critical. For now, the base case stays near 44% over the 30d. Actors absorb the current evidence mix without a decisive break toward either shock or relief.
foil.perspective_strategic: Strait of Hormuz risk: critical is setting the strategic baseline, and the current 40% probability implies a live but not settled risk path.
foil.perspective_contrarian: The current 58% confidence level still leaves room for reversal. If that restraint persists, the forecast can move below the current 40% baseline. Actors prioritize containment and the system drifts toward stabilization unless new hard signals emerge.
foil.actor_lens: Commodity traders: Price whether stress in Middle East becomes durable over the 30d. Rebalance positions if the probability path moves away from 40%.

foil.title: Inflation and rates pressure from Eastern Mediterranean maritime disruption state
foil.scenario_short: Eastern Mediterranean maritime disruption state, with 45% pressure and 73% state confirmation, will feed inflation and rates pressure through shipping cost shock over the next 30 days, reinforced by 3 urgent critical...
foil.perspective_strategic: The 45% pressure and 73% state confirmation signals indicate a moderate but persistent shipping cost shock transmission to rates and inflation, with only 1 clustered situation suggesting narrow scope.
foil.perspective_contrarian: The 75% confidence level and lack of directly matched reporting on Eastern Mediterranean could prevent escalation, as the system drifts toward stabilization unless new hard signals emerge, per the contrarian branch projected at 48%.
foil.actor_lens: Commodity traders: Price whether stress in Eastern Mediterranean becomes durable over the 30d. Rebalance positions if the probability path moves away from 56%.
foil.top_signal: Eastern Mediterranean maritime disruption state is feeding inflation and rates pressure through shipping cost shock, with 45% pressure and 73% state confirmation

seismic-infrastructure.absence_reason: No product publishes a seismic forecast — earthquakes are not predictable, so there is no forecast/intel alternative to azimuth's observed USGS record. This is the honest inverse: for an observed natural-hazard fact, the sourced record IS the only trustworthy source.
